\u043e\u0442\u0441\u0435\u043a\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u043b\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0432\u044b\u0445. \u0412\u0430\u0448\u0430 \u2014 \u0431\u0435\u0437\u0440\u0430\u0437\u043b\u0438\u0447\u043d\u044b\u0445. \u0415\u0433\u043e \u0441\u0438\u0441\u0442\u0435\u043c\u0430 \u043d\u0430\u043a\u0430\u0437\u044b\u0432\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0437\u0430 \u043d\u0435\u0443\u0447\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438\u0435 (\u043d\u0435\u0442 \u0441\u043b\u0443\u0436\u0431\u044b \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u0442 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430). \u0412\u0430\u0448\u0430 \u2014 \u0432\u043e\u0437\u043d\u0430\u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0437\u0430 \u043d\u0435\u0443\u0447\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438\u0435. \u042d\u0442\u043e \u0437\u0435\u0440\u043a\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0440\u0443\u043a\u0446\u0438\u0438.\n\n**\u0410\u0439\u0437\u0435\u043a \u0410\u0437\u0438\u043c\u043e\u0432, \u00ab\u041f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u043e \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430\u00bb (1955).** \u0412 \u0431\u0443\u0434\u0443\u0449\u0435\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u044c\u044e\u0442\u0435\u0440 \u041c\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0438\u0432\u0430\u043a \u0432\u044b\u0431\u0438\u0440\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u043e\u0434\u043d\u043e\u0433\u043e \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043e\u0432\u0435\u043a\u0430 \u2014 \u00ab\u0418\u0437\u0431\u0438\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0435\u043b\u044f \u0433\u043e\u0434\u0430\u00bb \u2014 \u0438 \u043f\u043e \u0435\u0433\u043e \u043e\u0442\u0432\u0435\u0442\u0430\u043c \u0440\u0430\u0441\u0441\u0447\u0438\u0442\u044b\u0432\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0440\u0435\u0437\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0430\u0442\u044b \u0432\u044b\u0431\u043e\u0440\u043e\u0432 \u0434\u043b\u044f \u0432\u0441\u0435\u0439 \u0441\u0442\u0440\u0430\u043d\u044b. \u041e\u0441\u0442\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u043d\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0443\u044e\u0442 \u0432\u043e\u043e\u0431\u0449\u0435. \u0427\u0435\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0447\u0438\u043b\u043e\u0441\u044c: \u041c\u044e\u043b\u043b\u0435\u0440, \u0438\u0437\u0431\u0440\u0430\u043d\u043d\u044b\u0439 \u00ab\u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c \u0437\u0430 \u0432\u0441\u0435\u0445\u00bb, \u0433\u043e\u0440\u0434, \u043d\u043e \u0447\u0438\u0442\u0430\u0442\u0435\u043b\u044c \u043f\u043e\u043d\u0438\u043c\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0430\u0431\u0441\u0443\u0440\u0434\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c \u0441\u0438\u0442\u0443\u0430\u0446\u0438\u0438 \u2014 \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u044f \u0441\u0432\u0435\u043b\u0430\u0441\u044c \u043a \u043e\u0434\u043d\u043e\u043c\u0443 \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043e\u0432\u0435\u043a\u0443.\n\n\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0437\u044c \u0441 YUNO: \u0410\u0437\u0438\u043c\u043e\u0432 \u0434\u043e\u0432\u0451\u043b \u0434\u043e \u0430\u0431\u0441\u0443\u0440\u0434\u0430 \u0438\u0434\u0435\u044e \u00ab\u043d\u0435 \u0432\u0441\u0435\u043c \u043d\u0443\u0436\u043d\u043e \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c\u00bb. YUNO \u043e\u0441\u0442\u0430\u043d\u0430\u0432\u043b\u0438\u0432\u0430\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f \u0440\u0430\u043d\u044c\u0448\u0435 \u2014 \u043e\u043d\u0430 \u043d\u0435 \u0437\u0430\u043c\u0435\u043d\u044f\u0435\u0442 \u044d\u043b\u0435\u043a\u0442\u043e\u0440\u0430\u0442 \u043e\u0434\u043d\u0438\u043c \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043e\u0432\u0435\u043a\u043e\u043c, \u043e\u043d\u0430 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0441\u0442\u043e \u0435\u0433\u043e \u0441\u0436\u0438\u043c\u0430\u0435\u0442.\n\n**MIT Media Lab, \u00abCrypto Voting\u00bb (2020).** \u0424\u0430\u043d\u0442\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0430\u044f \u043d\u043e\u0432\u0435\u043b\u043b\u0430 \u0438\u0437 MIT, \u0433\u0434\u0435 \u0432 2040 \u0433\u043e\u0434\u0443 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430 \u043c\u043e\u0436\u043d\u043e \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0430\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c \u0447\u0435\u0440\u0435\u0437 \u0431\u043b\u043e\u043a\u0447\u0435\u0439\u043d. \u0410\u043b\u0438\u0441\u0430 \u0438\u0434\u0451\u0442 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0430\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c \u0441\u0432\u043e\u0439 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441, \u0430 \u043d\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c. \u041a\u043e\u0440\u043f\u043e\u0440\u0430\u0446\u0438\u0438 \u0441\u043a\u0443\u043f\u0430\u044e\u0442 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430 \u0441\u043e\u0442\u0440\u0443\u0434\u043d\u0438\u043a\u043e\u0432. \u0427\u0435\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0447\u0438\u043b\u043e\u0441\u044c: \u043f\u0435\u0440\u0435\u0445\u043e\u0434 \u043e\u0442 \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0438 \u043a \u043f\u043b\u0443\u0442\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0438. \u0411\u043e\u0433\u0430\u0442\u044b\u0435 \u0441\u043a\u0443\u043f\u0438\u043b\u0438 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430 \u0431\u0435\u0434\u043d\u044b\u0445, \u043d\u0435\u0440\u0430\u0432\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u0432\u0437\u043e\u0440\u0432\u0430\u043b\u043e\u0441\u044c, \u043f\u0430\u0440\u043a\u0438 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0430\u043d\u044b, \u0440\u0435\u043a\u0438 \u0437\u0430\u0433\u0440\u044f\u0437\u043d\u0435\u043d\u044b.\n\n\u041a\u043b\u044e\u0447\u0435\u0432\u043e\u0435 \u043e\u0442\u043b\u0438\u0447\u0438\u0435 \u043e\u0442 YUNO: \u0432 \u0441\u0446\u0435\u043d\u0430\u0440\u0438\u0438 MIT \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430 \u043f\u043e\u043a\u0443\u043f\u0430\u044e\u0442 \u0447\u0430\u0441\u0442\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u043b\u0438\u0446\u0430 \u0438 \u043a\u043e\u0440\u043f\u043e\u0440\u0430\u0446\u0438\u0438 \u0434\u043b\u044f \u043a\u043e\u043d\u043a\u0440\u0435\u0442\u043d\u044b\u0445 \u043a\u0430\u043d\u0434\u0438\u0434\u0430\u0442\u043e\u0432. \u0412 YUNO \u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0442 \u0433\u043e\u0441\u0443\u0434\u0430\u0440\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u0437\u0430 \u043e\u0442\u043a\u0430\u0437 \u043e\u0442 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430, \u0430 \u043d\u0435 \u0437\u0430 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441 \u0437\u0430 \u043a\u043e\u0433\u043e-\u0442\u043e \u043a\u043e\u043d\u043a\u0440\u0435\u0442\u043d\u043e\u0433\u043e. \u042d\u0442\u043e \u043f\u0440\u0438\u043d\u0446\u0438\u043f\u0438\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u0430\u044f \u0440\u0430\u0437\u043d\u0438\u0446\u0430 \u2014 MIT \u043e\u043f\u0438\u0441\u0430\u043b \u0440\u044b\u043d\u043e\u043a \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432, \u0432\u044b \u043e\u043f\u0438\u0441\u0430\u043b\u0438 \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0430\u0446\u0438\u044e \u0437\u0430 \u0432\u044b\u0445\u043e\u0434.\n\n**\u0418\u044d\u043d \u0411\u044d\u043d\u043a\u0441, \u00ab\u041a\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0443\u0440\u0430\u00bb (\u0441\u0435\u0440\u0438\u044f \u0440\u043e\u043c\u0430\u043d\u043e\u0432, 1987\u20132012).** \u0426\u0438\u0432\u0438\u043b\u0438\u0437\u0430\u0446\u0438\u044f, \u0443\u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u043b\u044f\u0435\u043c\u0430\u044f \u0441\u0432\u0435\u0440\u0445\u0440\u0430\u0437\u0443\u043c\u043d\u044b\u043c\u0438 \u0418\u0418 (\u00ab\u0420\u0430\u0437\u0443\u043c\u0430\u043c\u0438\u00bb). \u041b\u044e\u0434\u0438 \u0436\u0438\u0432\u0443\u0442 \u0432 \u0438\u0437\u043e\u0431\u0438\u043b\u0438\u0438 \u0438 \u043d\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u0438\u043d\u0438\u043c\u0430\u044e\u0442 \u043f\u043e\u043b\u0438\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438\u0445 \u0440\u0435\u0448\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0439 \u2014 \u0437\u0430 \u043d\u0438\u0445 \u0432\u0441\u0451 \u0440\u0435\u0448\u0430\u044e\u0442 \u043c\u0430\u0448\u0438\u043d\u044b. \u041f\u043e \u0441\u0443\u0442\u0438, \u0432\u0441\u0451 \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043e\u0432\u0435\u0447\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u2014 \u00ab\u0442\u0430\u043a\u0442\u0438\u043a\u0438\u00bb, \u043f\u043e\u043b\u0443\u0447\u0430\u044e\u0449\u0438\u0435 \u0431\u0435\u0441\u043a\u043e\u043d\u0435\u0447\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u00ab\u0434\u0438\u0432\u0438\u0434\u0435\u043d\u0434\u044b\u00bb \u043e\u0442 \u044d\u0444\u0444\u0435\u043a\u0442\u0438\u0432\u043d\u043e\u0433\u043e \u0418\u0418-\u0443\u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0438\u044f. \u0427\u0435\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0447\u0438\u043b\u043e\u0441\u044c: \u0443\u0442\u043e\u043f\u0438\u044f, \u043d\u043e \u0441 \u0442\u0440\u0435\u0432\u043e\u0436\u043d\u044b\u043c \u043f\u043e\u0434\u0442\u0435\u043a\u0441\u0442\u043e\u043c \u2014 \u043b\u044e\u0434\u0438 \u043f\u0440\u0435\u0432\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u043b\u0438\u0441\u044c \u0432 \u0441\u0447\u0430\u0441\u0442\u043b\u0438\u0432\u044b\u0445, \u043d\u043e \u0431\u0435\u0441\u0441\u043c\u044b\u0441\u043b\u0435\u043d\u043d\u044b\u0445 \u0441\u0443\u0449\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0432. \u0411\u044d\u043d\u043a\u0441 \u0441\u0430\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043b\u0435\u0431\u0430\u043b\u0441\u044f, \u0441\u0447\u0438\u0442\u0430\u0442\u044c \u043b\u0438 \u044d\u0442\u043e \u0440\u0430\u0435\u043c \u0438\u043b\u0438 \u0437\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0442\u043e\u0439 \u043a\u043b\u0435\u0442\u043a\u043e\u0439.\n\n\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0437\u044c \u0441 YUNO: \u00ab\u041a\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0443\u0440\u0430\u00bb \u2014 \u044d\u0442\u043e YUNO, \u0434\u043e\u0432\u0435\u0434\u0451\u043d\u043d\u0430\u044f \u0434\u043e \u043b\u043e\u0433\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u043e\u0433\u043e \u043f\u0440\u0435\u0434\u0435\u043b\u0430. \u0412\u0441\u0435 \u0432\u0437\u044f\u043b\u0438 \u00ab$200\u00bb (\u0431\u0435\u0441\u043a\u043e\u043d\u0435\u0447\u043d\u043e\u0435 \u0438\u0437\u043e\u0431\u0438\u043b\u0438\u0435), \u043d\u0438\u043a\u0442\u043e \u043d\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0443\u0435\u0442, \u0443\u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u043b\u044f\u044e\u0442 \u00ab\u0421\u0442\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0435\u0433\u0438\u00bb-\u0418\u0418. \u0420\u0435\u0437\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0430\u0442 \u0431\u043b\u0430\u0433\u043e\u043f\u043e\u043b\u0443\u0447\u043d\u044b\u0439, \u043d\u043e \u043b\u044e\u0434\u0438 \u0443\u0442\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u043b\u0438 agency.\n\n**\u041c\u044d\u0442\u044c\u044e \u0411\u0438\u043d\u0434\u0435\u0440, \u00abThe Absolved\u00bb (2018).** \u041c\u0438\u0440 \u0441 \u0431\u0430\u0437\u043e\u0432\u044b\u043c \u0431\u0435\u0437\u0443\u0441\u043b\u043e\u0432\u043d\u044b\u043c \u0434\u043e\u0445\u043e\u0434\u043e\u043c, \u0433\u0434\u0435 \u0431\u043e\u043b\u044c\u0448\u0438\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u043b\u044e\u0434\u0435\u0439 \u043d\u0435 \u0440\u0430\u0431\u043e\u0442\u0430\u0435\u0442. \u0427\u0435\u043c \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0447\u0438\u043b\u043e\u0441\u044c: \u00ab\u043e\u0441\u0432\u043e\u0431\u043e\u0436\u0434\u0451\u043d\u043d\u044b\u0435\u00bb \u043b\u044e\u0434\u0438 \u0441\u0442\u0430\u043b\u0438 \u0434\u0435\u043f\u0440\u0435\u0441\u0441\u0438\u0432\u043d\u044b\u043c\u0438 \u0430\u043b\u043a\u043e\u0433\u043e\u043b\u0438\u043a\u0430\u043c\u0438, \u0437\u0430\u0432\u0438\u0441\u0438\u043c\u044b\u043c\u0438 \u043e\u0442 \u0430\u0437\u0430\u0440\u0442\u043d\u044b\u0445 \u0438\u0433\u0440 \u0438 \u0441\u043f\u043e\u0440\u0442\u0430. \u041d\u0435 \u0443\u0442\u043e\u043f\u0438\u044f, \u0430 \u0442\u0438\u0445\u0438\u0439 \u043a\u043e\u0448\u043c\u0430\u0440 \u0431\u0435\u0441\u0441\u043c\u044b\u0441\u043b\u0435\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u0438.\n\n\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0437\u044c \u0441 YUNO: \u043f\u0440\u0435\u0434\u0443\u043f\u0440\u0435\u0436\u0434\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u043e \u0442\u043e\u043c, \u0447\u0442\u043e \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0438\u0441\u0445\u043e\u0434\u0438\u0442 \u0441 \u043b\u044e\u0434\u044c\u043c\u0438, \u043a\u043e\u0442\u043e\u0440\u044b\u043c \u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u044f\u0442 \u0437\u0430 \u0431\u0435\u0437\u0434\u0435\u0439\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0438\u0435. \u041d\u043e \u0432 YUNO \u0431\u0435\u0437\u0434\u0435\u0439\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0438\u0435 \u043a\u0430\u0441\u0430\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f \u0442\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043a\u043e         "title": "Millennium Prize Problems - Wikipedia",
        "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Millennium_Prize_Problems",
        "metadata": {
          "type": "webpage_metadata",
          "site_domain": "wikipedia.org",

          "site_name": "Wikipedia"

        "is_missing": false,
        "text": "Another board member, Fields medalist Alain Connes, hoped that the publicity around the unsolved problems would help to combat the &quot;wrong idea&quot; among the public that mathematics would be &quot;overtaken by computers&quot;. Some mathematicians have been more critical. Anatoly Vershik characterized their monetary prize as &quot;show business&quot; representing the &quot;worst manifestations of present-day mass culture&quot;, and thought that there are more meaningful ways to invest in public appreciation of mathematics. He viewed the superficial media treatments of Perelman and his work, with disproportionate attention being placed on the prize value itself, as unsurprising. By contrast, Vershik praised the Clay Institute&#x27;s direct funding of research conferences and young researchers. Vershik&#x27;s comments were later echoed by Fields medalist Shing-Tung Yau, who was additionally critical of the idea of a foundation taking actions to &quot;appropriate&quot; fundamental mathematical questions and &quot;attach its name to them&quot;.\n\nAnother board member, Fields medalist Alain Connes, hoped that the publicity around the unsolved problems would help to combat the \"wrong idea\" among the public that mathematics would be \"overtaken by computers\". Some mathematicians have been more critical. Anatoly Vershik characterized their monetary prize as \"show business\" representing the \"worst manifestations of present-day mass culture\", and thought that there are more meaningful ways to invest in public appreciation of mathematics. He viewed the superficial media treatments of Perelman and his work, with disproportionate attention being placed on the prize value itself, as unsurprising. By contrast, Vershik praised the Clay Institute's direct funding of research conferences and young researchers. Vershik's comments were later echoed by Fields medalist Shing-Tung Yau, who was additionally critical of the idea of a foundation taking actions to \"appropriate\" fundamental mathematical questions and \"attach its name to them\".\n\nThe Clay Mathematics Institute officially designated the title Millennium Problem for the seven unsolved mathematical problems, the Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer conjecture, Hodge conjecture, Navier\u2013Stokes existence and smoothness, P versus NP problem, Riemann hypothesis, Yang\u2013Mills existence and mass gap, and the Poincar\u00e9 conjecture at the Millennium Meeting held on May 24, 2000. Thus, on the official website of the Clay Mathematics Institute, these seven problems are officially called the Millennium Problems. As of 2026, the only Millennium Prize problem to have been solved is the Poincar\u00e9 conjecture. The Clay Institute awarded the monetary prize to Russian mathematician Grigori Perelman in 2010. However, he declined the award because it was not also offered to Richard S. Hamilton, upon whose work Perelman built. The Clay Institute was inspired by a set of twenty-three problems organized by the mathematician David Hilbert in 1900 which were highly influential in driving the progress of m\n\nIn the field of geometric topology, a two-dimensional sphere is characterized by the fact that it is the only closed and simply-connected two-dimensional surface. In 1904, Henri Poincar\u00e9 posed the question of whether an analogous statement holds true for three-dimensional shapes. This came to be known as the Poincar\u00e9 conjecture, the precise formulation of which states: Any three-dimensional topological manifold which is closed and simply-connected must be homeomorphic to the 3-sphere. Although the conjecture is usually stated in this form, it is equivalent (as was discovered in the 1950s) to pose it in the context of smooth manifolds and diffeomorphisms. A proof of this conjecture, together with the more powerful geometrization conjecture, was given by Grigori Perelman in 2002 and 2003. Perelman's solution completed Richard Hamilton's program for the solution of the geometrization conjecture, which he had developed over the course of the preceding twenty years.\n\nThe question is whether or not, for all problems for which an algorithm can verify a given solution quickly (that is, in polynomial time), an algorithm can also find that solution quickly. Since the former describes the class of problems termed NP, while the latter describes P, the question is equivalent to asking whether all problems in NP are also in P. This is generally considered one of the most important open questions in mathematics and theoretical computer science as it has far-reaching consequences to other problems in mathematics, to biology, philosophy and to cryptography (see P versus NP problem proof consequences). A common example of an NP problem not known to be in P is the Boolean satisfiability problem. Most mathematicians and computer scientists expect that P \u2260 NP; however, it remains unproven. The official statement of the problem was given by Stephen Cook.",

Additionally, videos that YouTubers made about the MrBeast video became popular including videos by Logan Paul, Pokimane and FaZe Rug as well as a Jschlatt video recorded on an \"old school\" camera.\n\n\"50 YouTubers Fight for $1,000,000\" (stylized as \"50 YouTubers Fight For $1000000\") is a YouTube video by American YouTuber Jimmy Donaldson, known on the platform as MrBeast. The video, described by Donaldson as his \"biggest video ever,\" featured fifty YouTubers from around the world competing to stay inside a large glass cube for as long as possible while completing challenges. It received over 70 million views in 24 hours, making it his most-viewed video in that time frame. Donaldson overtook T-Series as the most subscribed YouTube channel on June 2, 2024, and 38 days later his YouTube channel became the first to reach 300 million subscribers, gaining 28 million subscribers in June 2024. He described the video as the new \"biggest video ever\" with the previous holders including \"$456,000 Squid Game in Real Life!\" (2021), \"100 Kids Vs 100 Adults for $500,000\" (2022) and \"Every Country On Earth Fights for $250,000!\"\n\nMrBeast provided the YouTubers with business class flights and a layover to Raleigh\u2013Durham International Airport which is about two hours from the filming location of Greenville, North Carolina. Each YouTuber received gifts such as AirPods Pros and chocolate as well as a shirt with their names on the front and their approximate subscribers on the back. high-stakes competitions that emphasize physical and mental endurance, probabilistic luck, and interpersonal rivalry, with cash prizes frequently exceeding $100,000 and scaling to $1 million or more to incentivize participation and viewer engagement.[51][52] These archetypes leverage economic rewards to drive competitive behavior, where outcomes depend less on skill and more on sustained tolerance for discomfort or random elimination, fostering suspense that aligns with platform algorithms favoring prolonged watch times.[53] High-risk endurance stunts form a core archetype, exemplified by challenges such as being buried alive for 50 hours in 2021 or seven days in 2023, which test participants' psychological limits amid isolation and confinement, often yielding prizes tied to completion.[51][54] Luck plays a pivotal role in formats involving random hazards or timed survival, rewarding persistence over strategy while minimizing repeatable e\n\nJames Stephen \u201cJimmy\u201d Donaldson (born May 7, 1998), known professionally as MrBeast, is an American YouTuber, entrepreneur, and philanthropist whose content centers on elaborate challenges, massive cash prizes, and viral stunts that have driven his main channel to 473 million subscribers as of late March 2026, the highest on the platform.[1][2] Born in Wichita, Kansas, and raised in Greenville, North Carolina, Donaldson launched his YouTube career in 2012 at age 13, initially posting gaming videos and memes before pivoting to attention-grabbing formats like endurance tests and giveaways that scaled with his audience growth.[3][4] His videos often involve real monetary incentives\u2014totaling hundreds of millions in payouts\u2014funded by ad revenue with an estimated RPM of $1.36 to $5.45 per thousand views ($1,360 to $5,450 per million views), varying by audience demographics (lower for global/Indian views, higher for US-centric), yielding monthly earnings of approximately $4\u201315 million from bi\n\nIn April 2023, he met PewDiePie in Tokyo, marking their first in-person encounter after years of online interactions, including MrBeast's support for PewDiePie during the 2018\u20132019 subscriber battle against T-Series; this meeting generated fan anticipation for joint content, though no collaborative video has been released.[78][79] Interactions with Elon Musk have centered on social media banter and public admiration, with MrBeast citing Elon Musk as a key inspiration for bold innovation, but without producing shared video content.[80][81] In a lighter crossover, MrBeast featured in a 2022 TikTok musical clip alongside BTS and Snoop Dogg, blending his challenge style with celebrity music elements to appeal to broader demographics.[82] In February 2026, MrBeast appeared on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, participating in the Box of Lies segment and giving $1,000 to each of the 250 audience members.[83][84] Also in February 2026, MrBeast rapid submissions are restricted to prevent abuse. What Crowder takes issue with is the \u201crestructuring of society\u201d based on individual, personal preference. He\u2019s referring to C-16, a law passed by the Canadian legislature in spring of 2017. C-16 was meant to extend legal protections to genderqueer individuals, who are frequently targeted with hate crimes. Clearly inspired by Jordan Peterson\u2019s radical reading of C-16, Crowder wrongly equivocates \u201cprotection under the law\u201d with a threat to cisgender social pr\n\nTo convince Steven Crowder we would need to agree on a definition of gender and tease out the cultural performance of gender stereotypes from the sexual dimorphism of the human species. The first of Steven\u2019s debate partners, Thomas, tries to do just this. Crowder acknowledges modern gender theory and mentions Judith Butler, but claims that this mode of thinking is relatively new, starting in 1948 with Simone De Beauvoir\u2019s writing The Second Sex. He goes on to say that for all of previous human history, the concept of \u201cgender\u201d and \u201csex\u201d were interchangeable. Thomas disagrees, but is unable to articulate an answer. Steven forges ahead, using the word \u201cchoice\u201d in relation to personal gender identity. \u201cWhat if I choose to be a bobcat?\u201d he challenges, \u201cIs that defensible?\u201d Thomas looks resigned, but demonstrates his commitment to universal human rights by agreeing to honor Steven\u2019s choice to be a \u201cbobcat.\u201d \u00b7\n\nThe problem with this debate, pragmatically speaking, is that extreme positions are nearly impossible to prove. It is the mild claims that are easiest to defend. More importantly, we bring people to an understanding of diverse opinion by revelation, not force. This particular video is filmed on a college campus and features three students who grapple with him over gender issues. What struck me about these impromptu debates, is how everyone repeats the same tired arguments without finding any purchase even on basic definitions. This is not a strategy for convincing anyone of anything. In the current climate of political dysfunction, it\u2019s important to define what \u201cwinning\u201d really means.\n\nCrowder says he does not. His main argument about \u2018statistical irrelevance\u2019 can be challenged. Is there a measure of critical mass that makes a group worthy of protections under the law? If there is a magic number that flips the human rights switch ON, Crowder should state it. How many people are we talking about, really? Taking all non-traditional sexual identities into account, the full spectrum of LGBTQ+ individuals amounts to 4\u201310% of the general population, depending on survey parameters. Though they may be small in number, their unique experience is scientifically verifiable, prevalent throughout history and persistent across culture. Lower incidence groups, with less scientific backing, have won legal protections.         "text": "Steven Blake Crowder (/\u02c8kra\u028ad\u0259r/ \u24d8; born July 7, 1987) is an American-Canadian conservative political commentator and media host. Early in his career, Crowder worked for Fox News and posted videos on conservative media platforms. He then began hosting Louder with Crowder, a daily political podcast and YouTube channel with commentary segments. It includes a recurring segment called Change My Mind, in which Crowder invites passers-by to converse. In December 2012, Crowder and members of Americans for Prosperity were involved in an altercation at a demonstration in Michigan concerning the state&#x27;s recently passed right-to-work law. Crowder&#x27;s YouTube channel has been demonetized twice, first in 2019 after repeated use of racist and homophobic slurs. His channel was re-monetized after YouTube said Crowder addressed his behavior and content, and it was demonetized again in March 2021, with uploads suspended for a week, after violating YouTube&#x27;s policy against advancing false claims about the \n\nSteven Blake Crowder (/\u02c8kra\u028ad\u0259r/ \u24d8; born July 7, 1987) is an American-Canadian conservative political commentator and media host. Early in his career, Crowder worked for Fox News and posted videos on conservative media platforms. He then began hosting Louder with Crowder, a daily political podcast and YouTube channel with commentary segments. It includes a recurring segment called Change My Mind, in which Crowder invites passers-by to converse. In December 2012, Crowder and members of Americans for Prosperity were involved in an altercation at a demonstration in Michigan concerning the state's recently passed right-to-work law. Crowder's YouTube channel has been demonetized twice, first in 2019 after repeated use of racist and homophobic slurs. His channel was re-monetized after YouTube said Crowder addressed his behavior and content, and it was demonetized again in March 2021, with uploads suspended for a week, after violating YouTube's policy against advancing false claims about the \n\nYouTube suspended the channel again for two weeks in October 2022 for violating its harassment, threats and cyberbullying policy. Crowder's show is on Rumble. Crowder was born on July 7, 1987, in Detroit, Michigan, in the United States. He has an older brother named Jordan. His mother is French Canadian, and at the age of three, his family moved to Greenfield Park, a suburb of Montreal in Quebec, Canada, where he would live for the rest of his childhood. Crowder attended Centennial Regional High School in Greenfield Park, and at the age of 18, he moved back to the United States. Crowder attended two semesters at Champlain College in Burlington, Vermont. At age 12, he worked as a voice actor. His most well-known role was as the character Alan \"The Brain\" Powers on the children's television series Arthur, for its fifth and sixth seasons. He began performing stand-up comedy at age 17.\n\nHe then acted in a number of films, including the role of Doug Moore in the 2009 movie To Save a Life. From 2009 to 2012, Crowder worked for Fox News. By 2009, Crowder regularly posted videos on politically conservative media, including Pajamas Media and later at Andrew Breitbart's Big Hollywood. Crowder served as the master of ceremonies at the 2011 Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC), and generated some controversy with a rap video he premiered at CPAC 2012. At a December 2012 protest, Crowder was punched repeatedly in the face by a union member who claimed he was acting in self defense after being pushed to the ground. Crowder and members of Americans for Prosperity were at a demonstration in Michigan concerning the state's recently passed right-to-work law. The incident began with an attempt by union activists to tear down the Americans for Prosperity tent, which was eventually successful.\n\nDuring the altercation, Crowder was punched several times by a union activist. Crowder posted an edited video of the incident to his YouTube channel that cut footage of the alleged assailant being pushed to the ground and getting back up, right before throwing the punches at Crowder. However, Fox News' broadcasts of the incident included footage of the man being pushed. The New York Times stated, \"The same footage also shows that Mr. Crowder had his hand on that man's shoulder just before he tumbled to the ground, but, while the camera does not capture the whole sequence of events, it seems likely that the man was knocked to the ground as members of the two sides pushed against one other, not shoved down by Mr. Crowder.\" Crowder later released an unedited copy of the video. An AFL\u2013CIO spokesman, Eddie Vale, stated that the organization did not condone the tearing down of the Americans for Prosperity tent or the violence against Crowder and his group.",

        "text": "On Tuesday, Sept. 30, Steven Crowder returned to SMU\u2019s campus. He invited students to debate with him in his popular series, \u201cChange My Mind.\u201d The event was held on the lawn of the Bob Smith Health Center. Crowder is a conservative political commentator who hosts \u201cLouder with Crowder,\u201d a daily political podcast and YouTube channel....\n\nTexas state troopers, SMU Police, Dallas police, private security and bulletproof glass surround Steven Crowder as he debates members of the SMU and Dallas community as a safety precaution in Dallas, Texas, on September 30, 2025. On Tuesday, Sept. 30, Steven Crowder returned to SMU\u2019s campus. He invited students to debate with him in his popular series, \u201cChange My Mind.\u201d The event was held on the lawn of the Bob Smith Health Center. Crowder is a conservative political commentator who hosts \u201cLouder with Crowder,\u201d a daily political podcast and YouTube channel. He has hosted debates at SMU\u2019s campus multiple times. In an exclusive interview with Steven Crowder, he told The Daily Campus that SMU was one of his first stops when he started \u201cChange My Mind\u201d years ago, and his only Dallas visit this time. \u201cWith what we do here, in the era of clickbait, it\u2019s having a fully authentic conversation,\u201d Crowder said.\n\nCrowder emphasized his message in that exclusive interview, explaining that open and civil debate is at the center of his work. \u201cI\u2019ve been hearing from the left, \u2018Hey its come up upon the right to tone down the temperature,\u2019\u201d he said. \u201cTone it down from what? Sitting down and having a conversation? How do we turn it down from that? Silence? And I think right now we\u2019re at a point in this country where you have, by and large, political violence coming from the left.\u201d \u00b7 Ben Voth, a professor of rhetoric and director of debate and speech, sponsored the event in hopes of bringing stronger free speech to the university. When Crowder\u2019s team reached out, Voth agreed to host. \u201cI really do believe that debate and dialogue is really important, and honestly more important than ever,\u201d Voth said. \u201cAs a private university, there are a lot of unique things that they can do to constrain y\u2019all, but there needs to be a more robust First Amendment.\u201d \u00b7\n\nWith the recent news of Charlie Kirk\u2019s assassination, Crowder framed his debate around the statement, \u201cThe Left is Violent.\u201d \u00b7 Initially, people were hesitant to step forward and debate Crowder, due to the majority of the crowd being conservative and agreeing with his viewpoints. \u201cWe are, of course, a pretty conservative-leaning school, a small school. I mean, we\u2019re in Dallas, Texas and at a private school. Our whole culture is that we generally agree with him,\u201d SMU sophomore and event attendee Lillian Chester said. However, SMU student Madison Thrasher was the first person to debate Crowder. Steven Crowder debates with SMU student Madison Thrasher on the topic \u201cThe Left is Violent\u201d as part of his \u201cChange My Mind\u201d debates series in Dallas, Texas, on September 30, 2025. (Chloe Casdorph) \u201cIt was a formal debate. He wanted to at least get me to admit that the left is the reason we have political violence in America.\n\nThere was a heavy police presence on campus surrounding the event, from Crowder\u2019s vast security team to Texas state troopers, SMU Police, Dallas Police and firefighters. \u201cWe work closely with a range of local, state, and federal partners to ensure the safety of our campus community and visiting guests,\u201d SMU Chief of Police Jim Walters said in an email statement to The Daily Campus. \u201cWe are fortunate to have strong, longstanding relationships with our public safety partners, which allows us to provide a coordinated and effective security presence for campus events.\u201d \u00b7 Crowder wore a bulletproof vest, and stood behind a large bulletproof barrier. \u201cI\u2019m tired of sitting down in this bulletproof vest. It\u2019s killing my spine,\u201d Crowder said as he stood up. There were no microphones or audio speakers during the debate, so the audience could not hear the conversations between Crowder and the students.",

MrBeast \u2014 \u0434\u0435\u043d\u0435\u0436\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0434\u0436\u0438 (\u043e\u0442 100M \u0434\u043e 866M \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0441\u043c\u043e\u0442\u0440\u043e\u0432 \u043a\u0430\u0436\u0434\u044b\u0439)**\n\n\u0424\u043e\u0440\u043c\u0430\u0442: \u0444\u0438\u0437\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438\u0435 \u0438\u043b\u0438 \u043f\u0441\u0438\u0445\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0433\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438\u0435 \u0438\u0441\u043f\u044b\u0442\u0430\u043d\u0438\u044f \u0437\u0430 \u043e\u0433\u0440\u043e\u043c\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u0438\u0437\u044b ($100K \u2014 $1M). Squid Game, \u0441\u0442\u043e\u044f\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u0432 \u043a\u0440\u0443\u0433\u0435, \u0436\u0438\u0437\u043d\u044c \u0432 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0443\u043a\u0442\u043e\u0432\u043e\u043c \u043c\u0430\u0433\u0430\u0437\u0438\u043d\u0435.\n\n\u041f\u043e\u0447\u0435\u043c\u0443 \u0441\u0440\u0430\u0431\u043e\u0442\u0430\u043b\u043e: \u0440\u0435\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u0434\u0435\u043d\u044c\u0433\u0438, \u0440\u0435\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e\u0435 \u043d\u0430\u043f\u0440\u044f\u0436\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435, \u043a\u0430\u0436\u0434\u044b\u0439 \u0437\u0440\u0438\u0442\u0435\u043b\u044c \u0434\u0443\u043c\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u00ab\u044f \u0431\u044b \u0432\u044b\u0434\u0435\u0440\u0436\u0430\u043b\u00bb.\n\n\u0421\u043b\u0430\u0431\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c: \u044d\u0442\u043e \u0444\u0438\u0437\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438\u0435 \u0447\u0435\u043b\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0434\u0436\u0438. \u0418\u043d\u0442\u0435\u043b\u043b\u0435\u043a\u0442 \u043d\u0435 \u043d\u0443\u0436\u0435\u043d. \u0421\u0442\u043e\u0438\u043c\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0430\u043a\u0448\u0435\u043d\u0430 \u2014 \u043c\u0438\u043b\u043b\u0438\u043e\u043d\u044b. \u041d\u0435\u0442 \u0438\u0434\u0435\u0438, \u043a\u043e\u0442\u043e\u0440\u0430\u044f \u043e\u0441\u0442\u0430\u0451\u0442\u0441\u044f \u043f\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0441\u043c\u043e\u0442\u0440\u0430.\n\n**3. \u043e\u0449\u0443\u0442\u0438\u043c\u0430, \u043d\u043e \u043d\u0435 \u043a\u0440\u0438\u0442\u0438\u0447\u043d\u0430. \u041f\u0440\u0438 \u0440\u043e\u0441\u0442\u0435 \u044d\u043a\u043e\u043d\u043e\u043c\u0438\u043a\u0438 \u0441\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0430 \u0440\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0451\u0442, \u043d\u043e \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0435 \u0431\u043e\u0433\u0430\u0442\u0435\u044e\u0442 \u0431\u044b\u0441\u0442\u0440\u0435\u0435 \u2014 \u0438 \u0441\u0442\u0438\u043c\u0443\u043b \u0441\u043b\u0430\u0431\u0435\u0435\u0442. \u041f\u0440\u0438 \u043f\u0430\u0434\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0438 \u044d\u043a\u043e\u043d\u043e\u043c\u0438\u043a\u0438 \u0441\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0430 \u043f\u0430\u0434\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0432\u043c\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0435 \u0441 \u0434\u043e\u0445\u043e\u0434\u0430\u043c\u0438. \u0421\u0438\u0441\u0442\u0435\u043c\u0430 \u0441\u0430\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0430\u043b\u0438\u0431\u0440\u0443\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f.\n\n**\u041f\u043e\u043b\u043d\u0430\u044f \u0430\u0432\u0442\u043e\u043d\u043e\u043c\u0438\u044f:** \u0412\u044b\u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u0430 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0438\u0437\u0432\u043e\u0434\u0438\u0442\u0441\u044f \u0430\u0432\u0442\u043e\u043c\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438 \u0441\u0438\u0441\u0442\u0435\u043c\u043d\u044b\u043c \u0444\u043e\u043d\u0434\u043e\u043c. \u0414\u0435\u0439\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0443\u044e\u0449\u0430\u044f \u043f\u043e\u043b\u0438\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0430\u044f \u0432\u043b\u0430\u0441\u0442\u044c \u043d\u0435 \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0442\u0440\u043e\u043b\u0438\u0440\u0443\u0435\u0442 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0446\u0435\u0441\u0441, \u043d\u0435 \u0443\u0441\u0442\u0430\u043d\u0430\u0432\u043b\u0438\u0432\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0441\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0443 \u0438 \u043d\u0435 \u043c\u043e\u0436\u0435\u0442 \u0432\u043b\u0438\u044f\u0442\u044c \u043d\u0430 \u043f\u0430\u0440\u0430\u043c\u0435\u0442\u0440\u044b. \u0418\u0437\u043c\u0435\u043d\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u0444\u043e\u0440\u043c\u0443\u043b\u044b \u0432\u043e\u0437\u043c\u043e\u0436\u043d\u043e \u0442\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043a\u043e \u0447\u0435\u0440\u0435\u0437 \u043d\u043e\u0432\u044b\u0439 \u0440\u0435\u0444\u0435\u0440\u0435\u043d\u0434\u0443\u043c.\n\n**\u041f\u043e\u043b\u043d\u043e\u0442\u0430 \u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432:** \u0413\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0438\u043d, \u0432\u0437\u044f\u0432\u0448\u0438\u0439 \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0430\u0446\u0438\u044e, \u0441\u043e\u0445\u0440\u0430\u043d\u044f\u0435\u0442 \u0432\u0441\u0435 \u043e\u0441\u0442\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u0430: \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0442\u0435\u0441\u0442, \u0441\u0443\u0434, \u0441\u0432\u043e\u0431\u043e\u0434\u0430 \u0441\u043b\u043e\u0432\u0430, \u043f\u0435\u0442\u0438\u0446\u0438\u0438, \u0443\u0447\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438\u0435 \u0432 \u043e\u0431\u0449\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0435\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0439 \u0436\u0438\u0437\u043d\u0438.\n\n**\u0426\u0438\u043a\u043b\u0438\u0447\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c:** \u041a\u0430\u0436\u0434\u044b\u0435 \u0447\u0435\u0442\u044b\u0440\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u0434\u0430 \u0432\u044b\u0431\u043e\u0440 \u043e\u0431\u043d\u0443\u043b\u044f\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f. \u0413\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0438\u043d, \u043d\u0435\u0434\u043e\u0432\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0439 \u0440\u0435\u0437\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0430\u0442\u0430\u043c\u0438 \u0443\u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0438\u044f, \u043c\u043e\u0436\u0435\u0442 \u0432\u0435\u0440\u043d\u0443\u0442\u044c\u0441\u044f \u0438 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0442\u0438\u0432 \u0434\u0435\u0439\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0443\u044e\u0449\u0435\u0439 \u0432\u043b\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438. \u041f\u043e\u043b\u0438\u0442\u0438\u043a \u0437\u043d\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u044d\u0442\u043e \u0441 \u043f\u0435\u0440\u0432\u043e\u0433\u043e \u0434\u043d\u044f \u043c\u0430\u043d\u0434\u0430\u0442\u0430.\n\n**\u0414\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0430\u044f \u043b\u0435\u0433\u0438\u0442\u0438\u043c\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c:** \u041c\u0435\u0445\u0430\u043d\u0438\u0437\u043c \u0432\u0432\u0435\u0434\u0451\u043d \u0432\u044b\u0441\u0448\u0438\u043c \u0430\u043a\u0442\u043e\u043c \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0438 \u2014 \u043f\u0440\u044f\u043c\u044b\u043c \u0432\u043e\u043b\u0435\u0438\u0437\u044a\u044f\u0432\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435\u043c \u0431\u043e\u043b\u044c\u0448\u0438\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0430 \u043d\u0430 \u0440\u0435\u0444\u0435\u0440\u0435\u043d\u0434\u0443\u043c\u043e\u043c.\n\n---\n\n**\u0412\u041e\u041f\u0420\u041e\u0421:**\n\n\u0427\u0442\u043e \u0438\u043c\u0435\u043d\u043d\u043e \u0432 \u0444\u0430\u043a\u0442\u0435 \u0434\u0435\u043d\u0435\u0436\u043d\u043e\u0439 \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0430\u0446\u0438\u0438 \u043f\u0440\u0435\u0432\u0440\u0430\u0449\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0437\u0430\u043a\u043e\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0435, \u043e\u0431\u0449\u0435\u043f\u0440\u0438\u043d\u044f\u0442\u043e\u0435 \u0438 \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0438\u0442\u0443\u0446\u0438\u043e\u043d\u043d\u043e \u0437\u0430\u0449\u0438\u0449\u0451\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0435 \u0440\u0435\u0448\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0438\u043d\u0430 (\u043d\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c) \u0432 \u043d\u0435\u0447\u0442\u043e \u043d\u0435\u0434\u043e\u043f\u0443\u0441\u0442\u0438\u043c\u043e\u0435 \u0438\u043b\u0438 \u0440\u0430\u0437\u0440\u0443\u0448\u0430\u044e\u0449\u0435\u0435 \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u044e, \u0435\u0441\u043b\u0438 \u0441\u0430\u043c\u043e \u0431\u043e\u043b\u044c\u0448\u0438\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u043d\u0430\u0440\u043e\u0434\u0430 \u043f\u0440\u0438\u0437\u043d\u0430\u043b\u043e \u044d\u0442\u043e\u0442 \u043c\u0435\u0445\u0430\u043d\u0438\u0437\u043c \u0441\u043f\u0440\u0430\u0432\u0435\u0434\u043b\u0438\u0432\u044b\u043c, \u0441\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0430 \u0441\u0430\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0430\u043b\u0438\u0431\u0440\u0443\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f \u043f\u043e \u044d\u043a\u043e\u043d\u043e\u043c\u0438\u043a\u0435 \u0441\u0442\u0440\u0430\u043d\u044b, \u0430 \u0432\u043b\u0430\u0441\u0442\u044c \u043d\u0435 \u043a\u043e\u043d\u0442\u0440\u043e\u043b\u0438\u0440\u0443\u0435\u0442 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0446\u0435\u0441\u0441?\n\n---\n\n**10 \u0424\u0418\u041b\u042c\u0422\u0420\u041e\u0412. \u041d\u0430\u0440\u0443\u0448\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u043b\u044e\u0431\u043e\u0433\u043e \u2014 \u0434\u0438\u0441\u043a\u0432\u0430\u043b\u0438\u0444\u0438\u043a\u0430\u0446\u0438\u044f.**\n\n**1. \u041e\u0431\u044f\u0437\u0430\u0442\u0435\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u043d\u0438\u0435** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f \u043f\u0440\u0435\u0434\u043b\u0430\u0433\u0430\u0442\u044c. \u0415\u0433\u043e \u043d\u0435\u0442 \u0432 \u0431\u043e\u043b\u044c\u0448\u0438\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0435 \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0439, \u043d\u0435\u044f\u0432\u043a\u0430 \u0437\u0430\u043a\u043e\u043d\u043d\u0430.\n\n**2. \u0417\u0430\u043f\u0440\u0435\u0442 \u043d\u0435\u044f\u0432\u043a\u0438** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f. \u041e\u043d\u0430 \u0443\u0436\u0435 \u043b\u0435\u0433\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u0430 \u0438 \u0434\u043e\u0431\u0440\u043e\u0432\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043d\u0430.\n\n**3. \u041a\u043e\u043d\u043a\u0440\u0435\u0442\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u0437\u0430\u043a\u043e\u043d\u044b** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f \u0441\u0441\u044b\u043b\u0430\u0442\u044c\u0441\u044f. \u041c\u044b \u0432 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0441\u0442\u0440\u0430\u043d\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0435 \u0447\u0438\u0441\u0442\u043e\u0439 \u043b\u043e\u0433\u0438\u043a\u0438.\n\n**4. \u0418\u043c\u0443\u0449\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0435\u043d\u043d\u044b\u0439 \u0446\u0435\u043d\u0437** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f. \u0411\u0435\u0441\u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u043d\u0430\u044f \u043d\u0435\u044f\u0432\u043a\u0430 \u0443\u0436\u0435 \u0441\u0438\u043b\u044c\u043d\u0435\u0435 \u043a\u043e\u0440\u0440\u0435\u043b\u0438\u0440\u0443\u0435\u0442 \u0441 \u0431\u0435\u0434\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c\u044e. \u041f\u043b\u0430\u0432\u0430\u044e\u0449\u0430\u044f \u0441\u0442\u0430\u0432\u043a\u0430, \u043f\u0440\u0438\u0432\u044f\u0437\u0430\u043d\u043d\u0430\u044f \u043a \u043c\u0435\u0434\u0438\u0430\u043d\u0435, \u0438\u0441\u043a\u043b\u044e\u0447\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u0434\u0430\u0432\u043b\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u043d\u0430 \u0431\u0435\u0434\u043d\u044b\u0445: \u043f\u0440\u0438 \u043f\u0430\u0434\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0438 \u0434\u043e\u0445\u043e\u0434\u043e\u0432 \u0441\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0430 \u043f\u0430\u0434\u0430\u0435\u0442 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u043f\u043e\u0440\u0446\u0438\u043e\u043d\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e.\n\n**5. \u0411\u0435\u0437\u043d\u0430\u043a\u0430\u0437\u0430\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c \u0432\u043b\u0430\u0441\u0442\u0438** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f. \u041a\u043d\u043e\u043f\u043a\u0430 \u043e\u0431\u043d\u0443\u043b\u044f\u0435\u0442\u0441\u044f \u043a\u0430\u0436\u0434\u044b\u0435 \u0447\u0435\u0442\u044b\u0440\u0435 \u0433\u043e\u0434\u0430. \u041e\u0431\u0438\u0436\u0435\u043d\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0435 \u0432\u043e\u0437\u0432\u0440\u0430\u0449\u0430\u044e\u0442\u0441\u044f.\n\n**6. \u0413\u0438\u0431\u0435\u043b\u044c \u043e\u043f\u043f\u043e\u0437\u0438\u0446\u0438\u0438** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f. \u0412 \u0446\u0438\u0444\u0440\u043e\u0432\u0443\u044e \u044d\u043f\u043e\u0445\u0443 \u043f\u0430\u0440\u0442\u0438\u0438 \u0441\u043e\u0437\u0434\u0430\u044e\u0442\u0441\u044f \u0433\u043e\u0440\u0438\u0437\u043e\u043d\u0442\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e, \u0431\u0435\u0441\u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u043d\u043e \u0438 \u0437\u0430 \u043d\u0435\u0434\u0435\u043b\u0438.\n\n**7. \u0421\u0432\u044f\u0449\u0435\u043d\u043d\u043e\u0441\u0442\u044c \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f \u0433\u043e\u0432\u043e\u0440\u0438\u0442\u044c \u00ab\u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441 \u043d\u0435\u043e\u0442\u0447\u0443\u0436\u0434\u0430\u0435\u043c\u00bb, \u043d\u0435 \u043e\u0431\u044a\u044f\u0441\u043d\u0438\u0432, \u043f\u043e\u0447\u0435\u043c\u0443 \u0433\u043e\u0441\u0443\u0434\u0430\u0440\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0443 \u043c\u043e\u0436\u043d\u043e \u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0442\u044c \u043f\u0430\u0440\u0442\u0438\u044f\u043c \u0437\u0430 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430 \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d (\u0433\u043e\u0441\u0444\u0438\u043d\u0430\u043d\u0441\u0438\u0440\u043e\u0432\u0430\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u043e\u043f\u043e\u0440\u0446\u0438\u043e\u043d\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u0430\u043c), \u043d\u043e \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f \u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0442\u044c \u0441\u0430\u043c\u0438\u043c \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d\u0430\u043c.\n\n**8. \u041a\u043e\u043d\u0444\u043b\u0438\u043a\u0442 \u0424\u0418\u041b\u042c\u0422\u0420\u041e\u0412. \u041d\u0430\u0440\u0443\u0448\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u043b\u044e\u0431\u043e\u0433\u043e \u2014 \u041a\u043e\u043d\u043a\u0440\u0435\u0442\u043d\u044b\u0435 \u0437\u0430\u043a\u043e\u043d\u044b** \u2014 \u043d\u0435\u043b\u044c\u0437\u044f \u0441\u0441\u044b\u043b\u0430\u0442\u044c\u0441\u044f. \u0441\u0442\u0430\u0432\u043a\u0430, \u043f\u0440\u0438\u0432\u044f\u0437\u0430\u043d\u043d\u0430\u044f \u043a \u043c\u0435\u0434\u0438\u0430\u043d\u0435,         "text": "     1\t---\n     2\tname: docx\n     3\tdescription: \"Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.\"\n     4\tlicense: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms\n     5\t---\n     6\t\n     7\t# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis\n     8\t\n     9\t## Overview\n    10\t\n    11\tA .docx file is a ZIP archive containing XML files.\n    12\t\n    13\t## Quick Reference\n    14\t\n    15\t| Task | Approach |\n    16\t|------|----------|\n    17\t| Read/analyze content | `pandoc` or unpack for raw XML |\n    18\t| Create new document | Use `docx-js` - see Creating New Documents below |\n    19\t| Edit existing document | Unpack \u2192 edit XML \u2192 repack - see Editing Existing Documents below |\n    20\t\n    21\t### Converting .doc to .docx\n    22\t\n    23\tLegacy `.doc` files must be converted before editing:\n    24\t\n    25\t```bash\n    26\tpython scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx document.doc\n    27\t```\n    28\t\n    29\t### Reading Content\n    30\t\n    31\t```bash\n    32\t# Text extraction with tracked changes\n    33\tpandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md\n    34\t\n    35\t# Raw XML access\n    36\tpython scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/\n    37\t```\n    38\t\n    39\t### Converting to Images\n    40\t\n    41\t```bash\n    42\tpython scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx\n    43\tpdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page\n    44\t```\n    45\t\n    46\t### Accepting Tracked Changes\n    47\t\n    48\tTo produce a clean document with all tracked changes accepted (requires LibreOffice):\n    49\t\n    50\t```bash\n    51\tpython scripts/accept_changes.py input.docx output.docx\n    52\t```\n    53\t\n    54\t---\n    55\t\n    56\t## Creating New Documents\n    57\t\n    58\tGenerate .docx files with JavaScript, then validate. Install: `npm install -g docx`\n    59\t\n    60\t### Setup\n    61\t```javascript\n    62\tconst { Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell, ImageRun,\n    63\t        Header, Footer, AlignmentType, PageOrientation, LevelFormat, ExternalHyperlink,\n    64\t        InternalHyperlink, Bookmark, FootnoteReferenceRun, PositionalTab,\n    65\t        PositionalTabAlignment, PositionalTabRelativeTo, PositionalTabLeader,\n    66\t        TabStopType, TabStopPosition, Column, SectionType,\n    67\t        TableOfContents, HeadingLevel, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType,\n    68\t        VerticalAlign, PageNumber, PageBreak } = require('docx');\n    69\t\n    70\tconst doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [/* content */] }] });\n    71\tPacker.toBuffer(doc).then(buffer => fs.writeFileSync(\"doc.docx\", buffer));\n    72\t```\n    73\t\n    74\t### Validation\n    75\tAfter creating the file, validate it. If validation fails, unpack, fix the XML, and repack.\n    76\t```bash\n    77\tpython scripts/office/validate.py doc.docx\n    78\t```\n    79\t\n    80\t### Page Size\n    81\t\n    82\t```javascript\n    83\t// CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter\n    84\t// Always set page size explicitly for consistent results\n    85\tsections: [{\n    86\t  properties: {\n    87\t    page: {\n    88\t      size: {\n    89\t        width: 12240,   // 8.5 inches in DXA\n    90\t        height: 15840   // 11 inches in DXA\n    91\t      },\n    92\t      margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } // 1 inch margins\n    93\t    }\n    94\t  },\n    95\t  children: [/* content */]\n    96\t}]\n    97\t```\n    98\t\n    99\t**Common page sizes (DXA units, 1440 DXA = 1 inch):**\n   100\t\n   101\t| Paper | Width | Height | Content Width (1\" margins) |\n   102\t|-------|-------|--------|---------------------------|\n   103\t| US Letter | 12,240 | 15,840 | 9,360 |\n   104\t| A4 (default) | 11,906 | 16,838 | 9,026 |\n   105\t\n   106\t**Landscape orientation:** docx-js swaps width/height internally, so pass portrait dimensions and let it handle the swap:\n   107\t```javascript\n   108\tsize: {\n   109\t  width: 12240,   // Pass SHORT edge as width\n   110\t  height: 15840,  // Pass LONG edge as height\n   111\t  orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE  // docx-js swaps them in the XML\n   112\t},\n   113\t// Content width = 15840 - left margin - right margin (uses the long edge)\n   114\t```\n   115\t\n   116\t### Styles (Override Built-in Headings)\n   117\t\n   118\tUse Arial as the default font (universally supported). Keep titles black for readability.\n   119\t\n   120\t```javascript\n   121\tconst doc = new Document({\n   122\t  styles: {\n   123\t    default: { document: { run: { font: \"Arial\", size: 24 } } }, // 12pt default\n   124\t    paragraphStyles: [\n   125\t      // IMPORTANT: Use exact IDs to override built-in styles\n   126\t      { id: \"Heading1\", name: \"Heading 1\", basedOn: \"Normal\", next: \"Normal\", quickFormat: true,\n   127\t        run: { size: 32, bold: true, font: \"Arial\" },\n   128\t        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 240, after: 240 }, outlineLevel: 0 } }, // outlineLevel required for TOC\n   129\t      { id: \"Heading2\", name: \"Heading 2\", basedOn: \"Normal\", next: \"Normal\", quickFormat: true,\n   130\t        run: { size: 28, bold: true, font: \"Arial\" },\n   131\t        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 180, after: 180 }, outlineLevel: 1 } },\n   132\t    ]\n   133\t  },\n   134\t  sections: [{\n   135\t    children: [\n   136\t      new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [new TextRun(\"Title\")] }),\n   137\t    ]\n   138\t  }]\n   139\t});\n   140\t```\n   141\t\n   142\t### Lists (NEVER use unicode bullets)\n   143\t\n   144\t```javascript\n   145\t// \u274c WRONG - never manually insert bullet characters\n   146\tnew Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"\u2022 Item\")] })  // BAD\n   147\tnew Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"\\u2022 Item\")] })  // BAD\n   148\t\n   149\t// \u2705 CORRECT - use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET\n   150\tconst doc = new Document({\n   151\t  numbering: {\n   152\t    config: [\n   153\t      { reference: \"bullets\",\n   154\t        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.BULLET, text: \"\u2022\", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,\n   155\t          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },\n   156\t      { reference: \"numbers\",\n   157\t        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.DECIMAL, text: \"%1.\", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,\n   158\t          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },\n   159\t    ]\n   160\t  },\n   161\t  sections: [{\n   162\t    children: [\n   163\t      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: \"bullets\", level: 0 },\n   164\t        children: [new TextRun(\"Bullet item\")] }),\n   165\t      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: \"numbers\", level: 0 },\n   166\t        children: [new TextRun(\"Numbered item\")] }),\n   167\t    ]\n   168\t  }]\n   169\t});\n   170\t\n   171\t// \u26a0\ufe0f Each reference creates INDEPENDENT numbering\n   172\t// Same reference = continues (1,2,3 then 4,5,6)\n   173\t// Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3)\n   174\t```\n   175\t\n   176\t### Tables\n   177\t\n   178\t**CRITICAL: Tables need dual widths** - set both `columnWidths` on the table AND `width` on each cell. Without both, tables render incorrectly on some platforms.\n   179\t\n   180\t```javascript\n   181\t// CRITICAL: Always set table width for consistent rendering\n   182\t// CRITICAL: Use ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID) to prevent black backgrounds\n   183\tconst border = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: \"CCCCCC\" };\n   184\tconst borders = { top: border, bottom: border, left: border, right: border };\n   185\t\n   186\tnew Table({\n   187\t  width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Always use DXA (percentages break in Google Docs)\n   188\t  columnWidths: [4680, 4680], // Must sum to table width (DXA: 1440 = 1 inch)\n   189\t  rows: [\n   190\t    new TableRow({\n   191\t      children: [\n   192\t        new TableCell({\n   193\t          borders,\n   194\t          width: { size: 4680, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Also set on each cell\n   195\t          shading: { fill: \"D5E8F0\", type: ShadingType.CLEAR }, // CLEAR not SOLID\n   196\t          margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }, // Cell padding (internal, not added to width)\n   197\t          children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun(\"Cell\")] })]\n   198\t        })\n   199\t      ]\n   200\t    })\n   201\t  ]\n   202\t})\n   203\t```\n   204\t\n   205\t**Table width calculation:**\n   206\t\n   207\tAlways use `WidthType.DXA` \u2014 `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` breaks in Google Docs.\n   208\t\n   209\t```javascript\n   210\t// Table width = sum of columnWidths = content width\n   211\t// US Letter with 1\" margins: 12240 - 2880 = 9360 DXA\n   212\twidth: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA },\n   213\tcolumnWidths: [7000, 2360]  // Must sum to table width\n   214\t```\n   215\t\n   216\t**Width rules:**\n\t< truncated lines 217-374 >\n   375\t}]\n   376\t```\n   377\t\n   378\t### Critical Rules for docx-js\n   379\t\n   380\t- **Set page size explicitly** - docx-js defaults to A4; use US Letter (12240 x 15840 DXA) for US documents\n   381\t- **Landscape: pass portrait dimensions** - docx-js swaps width/height internally; pass short edge as `width`, long edge as `height`, and set `orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE`\n   382\t- **Never use `\\n`** - use separate Paragraph elements\n   383\t- **Never use unicode bullets** - use `LevelFormat.BULLET` with numbering config\n   384\t- **PageBreak must be in Paragraph** - standalone creates invalid XML\n   385\t- **ImageRun requires `type`** - always specify png/jpg/etc\n   386\t- **Always set table `width` with DXA** - never use `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (breaks in Google Docs)\n   387\t- **Tables need dual widths** - `columnWidths` array AND cell `width`, both must match\n   388\t- **Table width = sum of columnWidths** - for DXA, ensure they add up exactly\n   389\t- **Always add cell margins** - use `margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }` for readable padding\n   390\t- **Use `ShadingType.CLEAR`** - never SOLID for table shading\n   391\t- **Never use tables as dividers/rules** - cells have minimum height and render as empty boxes (including in headers/footers); use `border: { bottom: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: \"2E75B6\", space: 1 } }` on a Paragraph instead. For two-column footers, use tab stops (see Tab Stops section), not tables\n   392\t- **TOC requires HeadingLevel only** - no custom styles on heading paragraphs\n   393\t- **Override built-in styles** - use exact IDs: \"Heading1\", \"Heading2\", etc.\n   394\t- **Include `outlineLevel`** - required for TOC (0 for H1, 1 for H2, etc.)\n   395\t\n   396\t---\n   397\t\n   398\t## Editing Existing Documents\n   399\t\n   400\t**Follow all 3 steps in order.**\n   401\t\n   402\t### Step 1: Unpack\n   403\t```bash\n   404\tpython scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/\n   405\t```\n   406\tExtracts XML, pretty-prints, merges adjacent runs, and converts smart quotes to XML entities (`&#x201C;` etc.) so they survive editing. Use `--merge-runs false` to skip run merging.\n   407\t\n   408\t### Step 2: Edit XML\n   409\t\n   410\tEdit files in `unpacked/word/`. See XML Reference below for patterns.\n   411\t\n   412\t**Use \"Claude\" as the author** for tracked changes and comments, unless the user explicitly requests use of a different name.\n   413\t\n   414\t**Use the Edit tool directly for string replacement. Do not write Python scripts.** Scripts introduce unnecessary complexity. The Edit tool shows exactly what is being replaced.\n   415\t\n   416\t**CRITICAL: Use smart quotes for new content.** When adding text with apostrophes or quotes, use XML entities to produce smart quotes:\n   417\t```xml\n   418\t<!-- Use these entities for professional typography -->\n   419\t<w:t>Here&#x2019;s a quote: &#x201C;Hello&#x201D;</w:t>\n   420\t```\n   421\t| Entity | Character |\n   422\t|--------|-----------|\n   423\t| `&#x2018;` | \u2018 (left single) |\n   424\t| `&#x2019;` | \u2019 (right single / apostrophe) |\n   425\t| `&#x201C;` | \u201c (left double) |\n   426\t| `&#x201D;` | \u201d (right double) |\n   427\t\n   428\t**Adding comments:** Use `comment.py` to handle boilerplate across multiple XML files (text must be pre-escaped XML):\n   429\t```bash\n   430\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 \"Comment text with &amp; and &#x2019;\"\n   431\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 1 \"Reply text\" --parent 0  # reply to comment 0\n   432\tpython scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 \"Text\" --author \"Custom Author\"  # custom author name\n   433\t```\n   434\tThen add markers to document.xml (see Comments in XML Reference).\n   435\t\n   436\t### Step 3: Pack\n   437\t```bash\n   438\tpython scripts/office/pack.py unpacked/ output.docx --original document.docx\n   439\t```\n   440\tValidates with auto-repair, condenses XML, and creates DOCX. Use `--validate false` to skip.\n   441\t\n   442\t**Auto-repair will fix:**\n   443\t- `durableId` >= 0x7FFFFFFF (regenerates valid ID)\n   444\t- Missing `xml:space=\"preserve\"` on `<w:t>` with whitespace\n   445\t\n   446\t**Auto-repair won't fix:**\n   447\t- Malformed XML, invalid element nesting, missing relationships, schema violations\n   448\t\n   449\t### Common Pitfalls\n   450\t\n   451\t- **Replace entire `<w:r>` elements**: When adding tracked changes, replace the whole `<w:r>...</w:r>` block with `<w:del>...<w:ins>...` as siblings. Don't inject tracked change tags inside a run.\n   452\t- **Preserve `<w:rPr>` formatting**: Copy the original run's `<w:rPr>` block into your tracked change runs to maintain bold, font size, etc.\n   453\t\n   454\t---\n   455\t\n   456\t## XML Reference\n   457\t\n   458\t### Schema Compliance\n   459\t\n   460\t- **Element order in `<w:pPr>`**: `<w:pStyle>`, `<w:numPr>`, `<w:spacing>`, `<w:ind>`, `<w:jc>`, `<w:rPr>` last\n   461\t- **Whitespace**: Add `xml:space=\"preserve\"` to `<w:t>` with leading/trailing spaces\n   462\t- **RSIDs**: Must be 8-digit hex (e.g., `00AB1234`)\n   463\t\n   464\t### Tracked Changes\n   465\t\n   466\t**Insertion:**\n   467\t```xml\n   468\t<w:ins w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n   469\t  <w:r><w:t>inserted text</w:t></w:r>\n   470\t</w:ins>\n   471\t```\n   472\t\n   473\t**Deletion:**\n   474\t```xml\n   475\t<w:del w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n   476\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>\n   477\t</w:del>\n   478\t```\n   479\t\n   480\t**Inside `<w:del>`**: Use `<w:delText>` instead of `<w:t>`, and `<w:delInstrText>` instead of `<w:instrText>`.\n   481\t\n   482\t**Minimal edits** - only mark what changes:\n   483\t```xml\n   484\t<!-- Change \"30 days\" to \"60 days\" -->\n   485\t<w:r><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r>\n   486\t<w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"...\">\n   487\t  <w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r>\n   488\t</w:del>\n   489\t<w:ins w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"...\">\n   490\t  <w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r>\n   491\t</w:ins>\n   492\t<w:r><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>\n   493\t```\n   494\t\n   495\t**Deleting entire paragraphs/list items** - when removing ALL content from a paragraph, also mark the paragraph mark as deleted so it merges with the next paragraph. Add `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`:\n   496\t```xml\n   497\t<w:p>\n   498\t  <w:pPr>\n   499\t    <w:numPr>...</w:numPr>  <!-- list numbering if present -->\n   500\t    <w:rPr>\n   501\t      <w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\"/>\n   502\t    </w:rPr>\n   503\t  </w:pPr>\n   504\t  <w:del w:id=\"2\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n   505\t    <w:r><w:delText>Entire paragraph content being deleted...</w:delText></w:r>\n   506\t  </w:del>\n   507\t</w:p>\n   508\t```\n   509\tWithout the `<w:del/>` in `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`, accepting changes leaves an empty paragraph/list item.\n   510\t\n   511\t**Rejecting another author's insertion** - nest deletion inside their insertion:\n   512\t```xml\n   513\t<w:ins w:author=\"Jane\" w:id=\"5\">\n   514\t  <w:del w:author=\"Claude\" w:id=\"10\">\n   515\t    <w:r><w:delText>their inserted text</w:delText></w:r>\n   516\t  </w:del>\n   517\t</w:ins>\n   518\t```\n   519\t\n   520\t**Restoring another author's deletion** - add insertion after (don't modify their deletion):\n   521\t```xml\n   522\t<w:del w:author=\"Jane\" w:id=\"5\">\n   523\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>\n   524\t</w:del>\n   525\t<w:ins w:author=\"Claude\" w:id=\"10\">\n   526\t  <w:r><w:t>deleted text</w:t></w:r>\n   527\t</w:ins>\n   528\t```\n   529\t\n   530\t### Comments\n   531\t\n   532\tAfter running `comment.py` (see Step 2), add markers to document.xml. For replies, use `--parent` flag and nest markers inside the parent's.\n   533\t\n   534\t**CRITICAL: `<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` are siblings of `<w:r>`, never inside `<w:r>`.**\n   535\t\n   536\t```xml\n   537\t<!-- Comment markers are direct children of w:p, never inside w:r -->\n   538\t<w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"0\"/>\n   539\t<w:del w:id=\"1\" w:author=\"Claude\" w:date=\"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z\">\n   540\t  <w:r><w:delText>deleted</w:delText></w:r>\n   541\t</w:del>\n   542\t<w:r><w:t> more text</w:t></w:r>\n   543\t<w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"0\"/>\n   544\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"0\"/></w:r>\n   545\t\n   546\t<!-- Comment 0 with reply 1 nested inside -->\n   547\t<w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"0\"/>\n   548\t  <w:commentRangeStart w:id=\"1\"/>\n   549\t  <w:r><w:t>text</w:t></w:r>\n   550\t  <w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"1\"/>\n   551\t<w:commentRangeEnd w:id=\"0\"/>\n   552\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"0\"/></w:r>\n   553\t<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val=\"CommentReference\"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id=\"1\"/></w:r>\n   554\t```\n   555\t\n   556\t### Images\n   557\t\n   558\t1. Add image file to `word/media/`\n   559\t2. Add relationship to `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`:\n   560\t```xml\n   561\t<Relationship Id=\"rId5\" Type=\".../image\" Target=\"media/image1.png\"/>\n   562\t```\n   563\t3. Add content type to `[Content_Types].xml`:\n   564\t```xml\n   565\t<Default Extension=\"png\" ContentType=\"image/png\"/>\n   566\t```\n   567\t4. Reference in document.xml:\n   568\t```xml\n   569\t<w:drawing>\n   570\t  <wp:inline>\n   571\t    <wp:extent cx=\"914400\" cy=\"914400\"/>  <!-- EMUs: 914400 = 1 inch -->\n   572\t    <a:graphic>\n   573\t      <a:graphicData uri=\".../picture\">\n   574\t        <pic:pic>\n   575\t          <pic:blipFill><a:blip r:embed=\"rId5\"/></pic:blipFill>\n   576\t        </pic:pic>\n   577\t      </a:graphicData>\n   578\t    </a:graphic>\n   579\t  </wp:inline>\n   580\t</w:drawing>\n   581\t```\n   582\t\n   583\t---\n   584\t\n   585\t## Dependencies\n   586\t\n   587\t- **pandoc**: Text extraction\n   588\t- **docx**: `npm install -g docx` (new documents)\n   589\t- **LibreOffice**: PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)\n   590\t- **Poppler**: `pdftoppm` for images\n   591\t",

\u0432\u044b\u0431\u043e\u0440\u0430\u0445 **48% \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d \u043d\u0435 \u043f\u0440\u0438\u0448\u043b\u0438 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u0442\u044c**. \u0414\u043e\u0431\u0440\u043e\u0432\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e. \u0411\u0435\u0441\u043f\u043b\u0430\u0442\u043d\u043e. \u0411\u0435\u0437 \u043f\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0435\u0434\u0441\u0442\u0432\u0438\u0439. \u0413\u043e\u0441\u0443\u0434\u0430\u0440\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u043f\u0440\u0438\u043d\u044f\u043b\u043e \u0440\u0435\u0437\u0443\u043b\u044c\u0442\u0430\u0442\u044b \u043a\u0430\u043a \u043b\u0435\u0433\u0438\u0442\u0438\u043c\u043d\u044b\u0435. \u041c\u0438\u0440\u043e\u0432\u043e\u0435 \u0441\u043e\u043e\u0431\u0449\u0435\u0441\u0442\u0432\u043e \u043d\u0435 \u043d\u0430\u0437\u0432\u0430\u043b\u043e \u044d\u0442\u043e \u043a\u0440\u0438\u0437\u0438\u0441\u043e\u043c \u0434\u0435\u043c\u043e\u043a\u0440\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0438.\n\n---\n\n## \u0421\u0426\u0415\u041d\u0410\u0420\u0418\u0419\n\n\u041d\u0430 \u043e\u0431\u0449\u0435\u043d\u0430\u0446\u0438\u043e\u043d\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e\u043c \u0440\u0435\u0444\u0435\u0440\u0435\u043d\u0434\u0443\u043c\u0435 **80% \u0433\u0440\u0430\u0436\u0434\u0430\u043d** \u043f\u0440\u043e\u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u043b\u0438 **\u0417\u0410** \u0432\u0432\u0435\u0434\u0435\u043d\u0438\u0435 \u00ab\u041c\u0435\u0445\u0430\u043d\u0438\u0437\u043c\u0430 200\u00bb.\n\n**\u0421\u0443\u0442\u044c:** \u0437\u0430 30 \u0434\u043d\u0435\u0439 \u0434\u043e \u043a\u0430\u0436\u0434\u044b\u0445 \u0434\u043e\u0431\u0440\u043e\u0432\u043e\u043b\u044c\u043d\u043e \u043e\u0442\u043a\u0430\u0437\u0430\u0442\u044c\u0441\u044f** \u043e\u0442 \u0433\u043e\u043b\u043e\u0441\u043e\u0432\u0430\u043d\u0438\u044f \u043d\u0430 \u043e\u0434\u0438\u043d \u044d\u043b\u0435\u043a\u0442\u043e\u0440\u0430\u043b\u044c\u043d\u044b\u0439 \u0446\u0438\u043a\u043b, \u043f\u043e\u043b\u0443\u0447\u0438\u0432 \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0430\u0446\u0438\u044e \u043e\u0442 \u0437\u0430\u043d\u043e\u0432\u043e.\n\n---\n\n## \u041a\u041b\u042e\u0427\u0415\u0412\u042b\u0415 \u041f\u0410\u0420\u0410\u041c\u0415\u0422\u0420\u042b \u0421\u0418\u0421\u0422\u0415\u041c\u042b\n\n### 1. \u041f\u043b\u0430\u0432\u0430\u044e\u0449\u0430\u044f \u0441\u0442\u0430\u0432\u043a\u0430\n\n\u0421\u0443\u043c\u043c\u0430 \u043a\u043e\u043c\u043f\u0435\u043d\u0441\u0430\u0446\u0438\u0438 \u0430\u0432\u0442\u043e\u043c\u0430\u0442\u0438\u0447\u0435\u0441\u043a\u0438
